 {"id":"caam-bgz","title":"Config schema: stealth section","description":"Add stealth configuration options to SPMConfig.

```yaml
stealth:
  enabled: false  # Master switch - all stealth features disabled by default
  
  delay:
    min_seconds: 30     # Minimum delay before switch completes
    max_seconds: 120    # Maximum delay (random between min-max)
    jitter_seconds: 30  # Additional random 0-N seconds added to delay
  
  cooldown:
    enabled: true       # Track and enforce cooldowns after limit hits
    codex_hours: 4      # Hours before codex account can be reused after limit
    claude_hours: 6     # Hours for claude
    gemini_hours: 4     # Hours for gemini
    default_hours: 4    # Fallback for unknown providers
  
  rotation:
    enabled: true       # Enable smart profile rotation
    prefer_healthy: true      # Weight toward profiles with fresh tokens
    avoid_recent_hours: 2     # Penalize profiles used in last N hours
    randomness: 0.2           # Random factor (0-1) in rotation scoring
```

## Validation Rules
- delay.min_seconds <= delay.max_seconds
- cooldown.*_hours >= 0
- rotation.randomness between 0 and 1

## Files
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add StealthConfig struct with nested structs
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:46:32.668918003-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:21:06.081885833-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:21:06.081885833-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented StealthConfig with SwitchDelay, Cooldown, and Rotation configs. All features opt-in (disabled by default). Added validation and comprehensive tests.","comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"caam-bgz","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - fix confusing enabled flag structure:

```yaml
stealth:
  switch_delay:
    enabled: false          # Master switch for this feature
    min_seconds: 5
    max_seconds: 30
    show_countdown: true

  cooldown:
    enabled: false          # Master switch for this feature
    default_minutes: 60
    track_limit_hits: true

  rotation:
    enabled: false          # Master switch for this feature
    algorithm: "smart"      # "smart" | "round_robin" | "random"
```

## Changes from Original Design

REMOVED - Top-level `enabled: false` that conflicted with per-feature `enabled` flags.

Rationale: The original design had:
```yaml
stealth:
  enabled: false  # <-- This was confusing
  switch_delay:
    enabled: false  # <-- Redundant when parent is disabled
```

Users would be confused: "Do I need to set both? What if parent is true but child is false?"

NEW DESIGN: Each feature has its OWN `enabled` flag. No parent switch.
- Want just delays? Set `switch_delay.enabled: true`
- Want just cooldowns? Set `cooldown.enabled: true`
- Want everything? Enable each one individually

This is clearer, more explicit, and avoids the confusing interaction between parent/child enabled flags.

## Files
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add StealthConfig struct (per-feature enabled)
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation

 {"id":"caam-bhcz","title":"E2E: Multi-machine sync workflow","description":"# E2E Test: Multi-Machine Sync Workflow

## Goal
Test synchronization between local and simulated remote machines.

## Note on SSH Testing
Full SSH testing requires network access. This test uses:
- Local file operations to simulate remote
- Mocked SSH client for connection tests only
- Real sync algorithms

## Workflow Steps

### 1. Setup
- Create local vault with profiles
- Create simulated remote vault (temp directory)
- Configure sync pool with simulated machine

### 2. Push Sync
```
# Local has newer token
modify local token expiry
sync push to simulated remote
verify remote matches local
```

### 3. Pull Sync
```
# Remote has newer token
modify remote token expiry
sync pull from simulated remote
verify local matches remote
```

### 4. Conflict Resolution
```
# Both modified
modify local token
modify remote token differently
sync auto  # should pick fresher token
verify correct token selected
```

### 5. Queue Retry
```
# Simulate failure
sync to unavailable machine
verify added to queue
retry queue
verify removed from queue on success
```

## Test File
internal/e2e/workflows/sync_workflow_test.go

## Logging Requirements
- Log freshness comparisons
- Log sync decisions (push/pull/skip)
- Log queue state
- Log conflict resolutions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Push sync works
- Pull sync works
- Conflicts resolved correctly
- Queue retry works
- Detailed logs for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:07:16.949058934-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T20:49:25.490821603-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T20:49:25.490821603-05:00","close_reason":"E2E multi-machine sync workflow tests implemented and passing. Tests cover: push sync (local fresher), pull sync (remote fresher), conflict resolution (picks fresher token), queue retry mechanism, and history tracking. Uses local file operations to simulate remote vault, with real sync algorithms.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-bhcz","depends_on_id":"caam-ldjp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:12:53.849841939-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-bi8","title":"Enhanced caam init: Guided setup wizard","description":"## Current State
`caam init` just creates directories. User still needs 5+ steps to be productive.

## Proposed Wizard
```
$ caam init

Welcome to CAAM! Let's set up instant account switching.

Detected tools:
  ✓ claude (logged in as user@example.com)
  ✓ codex (logged in as dev@company.com)  
  ✗ gemini (not installed)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Step 1: Save your current sessions

Save Claude auth as profile? [Y/n]: y
Profile name [user@example.com]: personal
✓ Saved claude/personal

Save Codex auth as profile? [Y/n]: y  
Profile name [dev@company.com]: work
✓ Saved codex/work

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Step 2: Add more accounts (optional)

Would you like to add another Claude account? [y/N]: y
Please login to Claude with your other account...
[Opens browser or shows instructions]
Profile name: work-claude
✓ Saved claude/work-claude

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Step 3: Shell integration (recommended)

Add to your shell config for seamless switching:
  eval "$(caam shell-init)"

Add this automatically? [Y/n]: y
✓ Added to ~/.zshrc

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Setup complete! You have:
  claude: personal (active), work-claude
  codex: work (active)

Quick commands:
  caam next claude     # Switch to next Claude profile
  caam status          # See all profiles and health
  caam activate        # Switch to specific profile

Happy coding! 🚀
```

## Features
- Auto-detect installed tools
- Detect current login state
- Guide through adding first profiles
- Offer shell integration
- Show quick reference at end","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:00:04.497953712-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T19:03:36.66814356-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T19:03:36.66814356-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of caam-bxq which has more detail","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-bi8","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:03:23.239033777-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"caam-blxo","title":"Implement real-time session token tracking","description":"# Task: Implement real-time session token tracking

## Overview
Track token usage during an active session in real-time, not just from historical logs. This enables accurate burn rate calculation even before logs are written.

## Why This Matters
Historical log parsing is great for:
- Cost analysis
- Long-term trends
- Post-session review

But for LIVE prediction during a session:
- Logs may not be written yet (buffered)
- Want sub-second burn rate updates
- Need to detect usage spikes immediately

## Technical Details

### Session Tracker
```go
// internal/usage/session.go

type SessionTracker struct {
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    entries     []TokenEntry
    windowSize  time.Duration
    
    // Callbacks
    onUsage     func(entry TokenEntry)
}

type TokenEntry struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Model       string
    InputTokens int64
    OutputTokens int64
    Source      string  // "api_response", "log_parse", "stream_estimate"
}

func (t *SessionTracker) Record(entry TokenEntry)
func (t *SessionTracker) BurnRate(window time.Duration) *BurnRateInfo
func (t *SessionTracker) TotalTokens() int64
```

### Integration with SmartRunner
```go
func (r *SmartRunner) handleAPIResponse(resp *Response) {
    // Extract usage from API response headers or body
    if usage := extractUsage(resp); usage != nil {
        r.sessionTracker.Record(TokenEntry{
            Timestamp:    time.Now(),
            Model:        resp.Model,
            InputTokens:  usage.InputTokens,
            OutputTokens: usage.OutputTokens,
            Source:       "api_response",
        })
    }
}
```

### Stream Token Estimation
For streaming responses, estimate tokens in real-time:
```go
func (t *SessionTracker) EstimateFromStream(chunk string) {
    // Rough estimation: ~4 chars per token for English
    estimatedTokens := len(chunk) / 4
    
    t.Record(TokenEntry{
        Timestamp:    time.Now(),
        OutputTokens: int64(estimatedTokens),
        Source:       "stream_estimate",
    })
}
```

### Merging with Log Data
```go
func (t *SessionTracker) MergeLogData(entries []*logs.LogEntry) {
    // Deduplicate based on timestamp/conversation_id
    // Prefer log data over estimates when available
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tracks tokens from API responses in real-time
- [ ] Estimates tokens from streaming output
- [ ] Calculates accurate burn rate from live data
- [ ] Merges with historical log data correctly
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Memory-efficient (rolling window, not unbounded)

## Test Cases
1. Record and retrieve token entries
2. Calculate burn rate from entries
3. Stream estimation accuracy
4. Merge with log data (deduplication)
5. Thread safety under concurrent writes

## Files to Create
- internal/usage/session.go
- internal/usage/session_test.go

## Dependencies
- Create BurnRateInfo struct (caam-ef8r)

 {"id":"caam-bvd","title":"TUI Backup Feature","description":"# Epic: TUI Backup Feature

## Overview
Add the ability to backup current auth for any coding CLI directly from the TUI, in addition to the existing CLI command.

## Background
Currently, `caam backup <tool> <email>` is CLI-only. Users who prefer the TUI interface must exit to CLI to backup. This creates friction in the workflow.

## User Story
As a user working in the TUI, I want to backup my current auth state without leaving the TUI, so that I can maintain my flow while managing profiles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Press a key (e.g., 'b') in TUI to initiate backup
- [ ] Text input dialog appears for entering profile name/email
- [ ] Backup executes using existing vault.Backup() logic
- [ ] Success/error feedback displayed in TUI
- [ ] Profile list refreshes automatically after backup
- [ ] Works for all providers (claude, codex, gemini)

## Technical Notes
- TUI uses Bubble Tea framework (internal/tui/)
- Existing backup logic is in internal/authfile/vault.go
- Need to add text input component (charmbracelet/bubbles has textinput)
- Consider: validate email format? Or allow any string?

## Dependencies
None - uses existing infrastructure

## Effort Estimate
Small - primarily TUI wiring and dialog component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:37.714128841-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:44:34.642714671-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:44:34.642714671-05:00","close_reason":"Epic complete: Text input dialog (caam-i5i) and TUI backup action (caam-7vg) both closed. All acceptance criteria met: 'b' key triggers backup, dialog prompts for profile name, validation works, backup executes with feedback, profile list refreshes. Tests passing."}
 {"id":"caam-bxq","title":"Enhanced init wizard with auth discovery","description":"## Overview
Transform the first-run experience from "read docs, run 5 commands" to "run one command, answer prompts, done." The init wizard discovers existing auth files, extracts identities, and guides users through setup.

## The Problem
Current first-time setup requires:
1. Read README to understand concepts
2. Ensure you're logged into each tool
3. Run `caam backup claude <email>` (must know email)
4. Repeat for each tool and account
5. Optionally configure shell integration

This takes 10+ minutes and requires understanding caam's mental model first.

## The Solution
```bash
$ caam init

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║           Welcome to CAAM - Account Manager               ║
║         Instant switching for AI coding tools             ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Scanning for AI tools and existing sessions...

Found:
  ✓ claude   ~/.claude.json (logged in as alice@gmail.com)
  ✓ codex    ~/.codex/auth.json (logged in as dev@company.com)
  ✗ gemini   not installed

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STEP 1: Save Current Sessions
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Save your Claude session as a profile?
This lets you switch back to it later.

Profile name [alice@gmail.com]: personal
✓ Saved claude/personal

Save your Codex session as a profile?
Profile name [dev@company.com]: work  
✓ Saved codex/work

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STEP 2: Add More Accounts (Optional)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Would you like to add another Claude account? [y/N]: y

To add an account, you'll need to:
1. Log out of Claude
2. Log in with the other account
3. Come back here

Ready? [Y/n]: y
Opening Claude login... (waiting for completion)

[User completes OAuth in browser]

Detected new session: bob@gmail.com
Profile name [bob@gmail.com]: work-claude
✓ Saved claude/work-claude

Restoring your original session (alice@gmail.com)...
✓ Restored claude/personal as active

Add another? [y/N]: n

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STEP 3: Shell Integration (Recommended)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Enable automatic account management?
This adds aliases so 'claude' and 'codex' use caam automatically.

Install shell integration? [Y/n]: y
Detected shell: zsh

Added to ~/.zshrc:
  eval "$(caam shell-init zsh)"

Run this now to activate:
  source ~/.zshrc

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✓ Setup Complete!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Your profiles:
  claude: personal (active), work-claude
  codex:  work (active)

Quick commands:
  caam status        See all profiles
  caam next claude   Switch Claude profile
  caam add codex     Add another Codex account

Happy coding! 🚀
```

## Technical Design

### Auth File Discovery
Scan standard locations for each provider:
```go
func DiscoverAuth(tool string) (*DiscoveredAuth, error) {
    spec := provider.Registry.Get(tool).AuthFiles()
    for _, f := range spec {
        if exists(f.Path) {
            identity, err := extractIdentity(tool, f.Path)
            return &DiscoveredAuth{
                Tool:     tool,
                Path:     f.Path,
                Identity: identity, // email or account ID
                Valid:    err == nil,
            }, nil
        }
    }
    return nil, ErrNoAuth
}
```

### Identity Extraction
Parse auth files to extract user identity:

**Claude** (`~/.claude.json`):
```json
{
  "oauthAccessToken": "...",
  "oauthRefreshToken": "...",
  "claudeAiSessionKey": "sk-ant-...",
  // Look for email in JWT claims or additional fields
}
```
- Decode JWT if present, extract email/sub claim
- Fall back to "unknown" if can't parse

**Codex** (`~/.codex/auth.json`):
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "user": {
    "email": "dev@company.com",
    "name": "Developer"
  }
}
```
- Direct field access

**Gemini** (`~/.gemini/settings.json`):
```json
{
  "account": {
    "email": "user@gmail.com"
  }
}
```
- Direct field access

### Wizard Flow Controller
```go
type WizardState struct {
    Discovered   []DiscoveredAuth
    SavedProfiles []string
    AddedAccounts []string
    ShellSetup   bool
}

func RunWizard() error {
    state := &WizardState{}
    
    // Phase 1: Discovery
    state.Discovered = discoverAllAuth()
    showDiscoveryResults(state.Discovered)
    
    // Phase 2: Save existing
    for _, auth := range state.Discovered {
        if promptSave(auth) {
            name := promptProfileName(auth.Identity)
            vault.Backup(auth.Tool, name)
            state.SavedProfiles = append(state.SavedProfiles, name)
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 3: Add more (optional loop)
    for {
        if !promptAddMore() {
            break
        }
        runAddAccountFlow()
    }
    
    // Phase 4: Shell integration
    if promptShellIntegration() {
        installShellInit()
        state.ShellSetup = true
    }
    
    // Phase 5: Summary
    showSummary(state)
    return nil
}
```

### Interactive Prompts
Using bubbletea or simple stdin for prompts:
- Yes/No prompts with smart defaults
- Text input with default values
- Progress indicators during operations
- Clear section headers

## Configuration Created
After wizard completes:
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
version: 1
defaults:
  claude: personal
  codex: work
shell:
  auto_aliases: true
  completion: true
```

## Edge Cases

### No auth files found
```
No AI tool sessions found.

Would you like to:
  [1] Log in to Claude now
  [2] Log in to Codex now  
  [3] Log in to Gemini now
  [4] Exit and set up later

Choice [4]: 
```

### Can't parse identity
```
Found Claude auth at ~/.claude.json
Could not determine account email.
Profile name: _____  (no default suggested)
```

### Existing vault profiles
```
Found existing CAAM profiles:
  claude: work, personal
  codex: main

Merge with new setup or start fresh?
  [M]erge - keep existing, add new
  [F]resh - backup existing, start over
  [C]ancel

Choice [M]:
```

### Multiple accounts already
```
You have 3 Claude accounts saved.
Would you like to add more? [y/N]:
```

## Flags
```bash
caam init              # Interactive wizard
caam init --quick      # Auto-save all discovered, skip prompts
caam init --shell=zsh  # Pre-specify shell type
caam init --no-shell   # Skip shell integration step
caam init --reset      # Clear existing and start fresh
```

## Implementation Tasks
1. **Auth discovery** (internal/discovery/scanner.go)
   - Scan auth file locations
   - Check file existence and readability

2. **Identity extraction** (internal/discovery/identity.go)
   - Parse each provider's auth format
   - JWT decoding for tokens
   - Graceful fallback on parse failure

3. **Wizard UI** (cmd/caam/cmd/init.go)
   - Section-based flow
   - Interactive prompts
   - Progress feedback

4. **Shell integration installer** (cmd/caam/cmd/shell_init.go)
   - Detect shell type
   - Append to rc file
   - Idempotent (don't duplicate)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Discovers auth for all three providers
- [ ] Extracts identity (email) when possible
- [ ] Saves discovered sessions as profiles
- [ ] Guides through adding additional accounts
- [ ] Installs shell integration optionally
- [ ] Works with no existing auth (guides to first login)
- [ ] Handles existing profiles gracefully
- [ ] Completes in <2 minutes for typical user

## Dependencies
- Vault.Backup() for saving profiles
- Provider auth file specs
- JWT parsing for identity extraction
- Shell init infrastructure (caam-k9z)

## Priority: P0 (Critical for Adoption)
First impressions matter.  {"id":"caam-by4","title":"Terminal UI (TUI) with Bubble Tea","description":"## Overview
A visual terminal interface for managing caam profiles. While the CLI is fully functional, a TUI provides:
- At-a-glance view of all profiles across providers
- Visual feedback on status (logged in, locked, active)
- Discoverable interface for new users
- Running session management

## Design (from original spec)
Layout:
- Left panel: Provider list (Codex / Claude / Gemini)
- Center panel: Profiles table (Name, Auth mode, Logged-in?, Last used, Notes)
- Right panel: Action pane + detail view for selected profile

Keybindings:
- ↑/↓: Select profile
- Enter: Run default command (launch CLI)
- l: Login/refresh auth
- o: Open account page in browser
- e: Edit profile (notes, browser config)
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter profiles
- Tab: Switch between providers
- q: Quit

## Implementation
Use Bubble Tea (bubbletea) with:
- bubbles for standard components (list, table, textinput)
- lipgloss for styling

## User Value
- Visual overview prevents mistakes (wrong account)
- Running sessions visible prevents data corruption
- Lower learning curve than CLI-only

## Consideration
TUI should be the default when running 'caam' with no args, but all functionality must remain available via CLI for scripting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:51:16.807075968-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:46:22.843793337-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:46:22.843793337-05:00","close_reason":"All TUI components implemented: scaffolding (caam-qpx), provider panel (caam-ta7), profiles table (caam-nmm), detail panel (caam-f9h), keyboard navigation (caam-j14). TUI is fully functional with three-panel layout and all keybindings wired up."}
